name: ValueSetRelated
title: Value Set Related Determination
description: Determine the relationship between two value sets. Different versions of code systems are considered compatible unless versionNeeded = true for the code system
comment: An $expand will be performed internally if needed.

parameter.name: thisUrl
parameter.documentation: Value set Canonical URL for the first value set of the pair (the base). The server must know the value set (e.g. it is provided as an attached resource, it is defined explicitly in the server's value sets, or it is defined implicitly by some code system known to the server

parameter.name: otherUrl
parameter.documentation: Value set Canonical URL for the second value set of the pair (the one being compared to base). The server must know the value set (e.g. it is provided as an attached resource, it is defined explicitly in the server's value sets, or it is defined implicitly by some code system known to the server

parameter.name: thisValueSet
parameter.documentation: The first value set is provided directly as part of the request. Servers may choose not to accept value sets in this fashion. This parameter is used when the client wants the server to expand a value set that is not stored on the server

parameter.name: otherValueSet
parameter.documentation: The other value set is provided directly as part of the request. Servers may choose not to accept value sets in this fashion. This parameter is used when the client wants the server to expand a value set that is not stored on the server

parameter.name: thisVersion
parameter.documentation: The identifier that is used to identify a specific version of the value set to be used when validating the code. This is an arbitrary value managed by the value set author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available.

parameter.name: otherVersion
parameter.documentation: The identifier that is used to identify a specific version of the value set to be used when validating the code. This is an arbitrary value managed by the value set author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available.

parameter.name: useSupplement
parameter.documentation: The supplement must be used when validating the code. Use of this parameter should result in $validate-code behaving the same way as if the supplements were included in the value set definition using the [http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/valueset-supplement](http://hl7.org/fhir/extensions/StructureDefinition-valueset-supplement.html)

parameter.name: diagnostics
parameter.documentation: Whether to return information about the reasoning process

parameter.name: result
parameter.documentation: The relationship between the ValueSets. One of: same, superset, subset, overlapping, dsjoint, empty, and indeterminate

parameter.name: message
parameter.documentation: Explanation of the code, with reason if appropriate
